# NGX API Mimic (NgxApiMimic)

> A powerful library to create backend-like API mocks under the HTTP Interceptor inside Angular applications.

- **Version**: 0.0.7
- **Author**: Mateusz Bilicz (https://mateuszbilicz.pl)
- **Repository**: https://github.com/mateuszbilicz/ngx-api-mimic
- **Website/Docs**: https://ngx-api-mimic.mateuszbilicz.pl
- **NPM Package**: ngx-api-mimic

## Description
This library allows developers to mock data and create a fake REST API under an HTTP Interceptor inside Angular applications.
The Mocking data functionality allows generating any type of data based on a schematic. Writing schematics is straightforward when using TypeScript interfaces, as the IDE will provide field hints. Mocked data can be configured to be automatically stored in the LocalStorage.

This library provides a complete toolset to create API mocks, including:
- Angular interceptor
- Router
- Controllers
- Endpoint decorators
- Guard decorators
- Pipes and Param decorators

## Distinct Features
Unlike generic HTTP interceptors, this library allows developers to mock data and the API simultaneously by using a `@UsingSchema(storeName?, schematic)` decorator on a Controller class.
The API design is heavily inspired by the **NestJS framework**. Consequently, developers can easily migrate their mock controller code directly into a real NestJS backend without having to rewrite significant portions of the API.

## Ideal Use Cases
- Small demo apps that do not require a fully functional backend yet.
- Recruitment tasks or homework assignments.
- Real-time portfolio applications.

## Warnings
*Do not use this library for production applications.* It operates purely as a frontend interceptor and is not a secure, functional backend server.
Some class or interface names within this library are identical to Angular's names. It is strongly advised to separate these mock classes into their own files/directories away from regular Angular components or services to avoid `import` naming conflicts.

## Installation
`npm install ngx-api-mimic`
